title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Bennett was diagnosed with Type 1 diabetes at age 11, inspiring his marathon participation.
He supports the Joslin Diabetes Center, which provided crucial guidance and confidence post-diagnosis.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter receives her fifth Oscar nomination, making her the most-nominated Black woman in Oscars history.
Carter won her first Oscar in 2019 for 'Black Panther' and made history again in 2023 with a second win.
Carter's costume design for 'Sinners' is tied to Massachusetts due to materials sourced from Brimfield Antique Marketplace.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face over 200 layoffs if a proposed property-tax override is not approved by voters.
Officials estimate $18.5 million needed over three years to maintain current staffing and programs in the district.
Brookline voters to decide on the override during the annual town election set for May 5.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The Massachusetts PETS Act aims to regulate the sale and housing of dogs and cats to improve animal welfare.
The bill proposes banning pet shop sales of dogs and cats, instead encouraging adoptions through animal shelters.
The legislation seeks to remove insurance and housing barriers limiting pet ownership, particularly in public housing.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: State officials urge water conservation in Massachusetts despite heavy winter snowfall due to ongoing drought conditions.
Central and Northeast Massachusetts have been elevated to Level 3 Critical Drought, impacting water resources.
Warm weather melting snow offers limited relief, contributing only 2.5 inches of water, insufficient to replenish water tables.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Women are rethinking public displays of heterosexual relationships, questioning the traditional emphasis on centering male partners.
Chanté Joseph's viral essay discusses why women may obscure their boyfriends on social media for greater independence.
The trend suggests broader questioning of societal norms around relationships and the perceived necessity of being partnered.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O'Neill is running the Boston Marathon to support The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
The camp provides medically safe, fun experiences for children with serious medical conditions.
O'Neill's fiancée's sister, a camp alum, is now a counselor after surviving cancer.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Eagles reportedly refuse to trade wide receiver A.J. Brown to the Patriots despite ongoing interest.
Patriots had reportedly offered first- and third-round picks for Brown, which the Eagles declined.
Trade talks might resume around June 1 when salary cap relief could make a deal more feasible.

title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum is cleared to play against the Washington Wizards after missing a game for injury management.
Tatum is playing under a minutes restriction as he continues to recover from a ruptured Achilles.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable, while Nikola Vucevic is out for the Celtics' matchup with the Wizards.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'War Machine' starring Alan Ritchson is streaming on Netflix, featuring a soldier tackling a deadly war machine.
'Zootopia 2' with Jason Bateman and Ginnifer Goodwin is now available on Disney+, just before the 2026 Oscars.
Steve Carell stars in 'Rooster' on HBO Max, a comedy about a divorced author starting anew on a college campus.
